items.title: FRC Refreshes Auditing Standards to Reduce Reporting Burden
items.summary: The FRC has revised three UK auditing standards (ISA (UK) 700, 701, 720) to make auditor's reports shorter, clearer and more useful to investors. The revised standards take effect from 15 December 2026.

items.title: FRC Publishes First UK Guidance on AI Use in Auditing
items.summary: The FRC has published what it describes as the world's first guidance on AI use in auditing, warning firms they remain accountable for AI-driven errors — as EY rolls out its AI-powered Canvas platform and KPMG pilots orchestration agents on Clara.

items.title: FRC Evolves Audit Supervisory Model
items.summary: A more proportionate, integrated supervisory framework began rolling out from April 2026 for the largest firms, placing Systems of Quality Management (SoQM) at the heart of supervision, supported by thematic reviews and corroboratory inspections.

items.title: UK Government Drops Audit Reform Bill
items.summary: In January 2026 the UK government announced it would not proceed with a standalone Audit Reform Bill, citing reduced administrative burden priorities and recent improvements in audit regulation as reasons major legislative reform is now less pressing.

items.title: IFRS 18 Comparatives Now Required for 2026
items.summary: Although IFRS 18 isn't mandatorily effective until January 2027, entities must apply it retrospectively — meaning 2026 comparative figures need to be prepared on an IFRS 18 basis now, alongside new IFRS 7/IFRS 9 amendments on settlement-date derecognition and ESG-linked cash flows.

items.title: IASB Chair Transition Raises Direction Questions
items.summary: The non-renewal of Andreas Barckow's term as IASB chair (concluding June 2026) has fuelled debate about the IFRS Foundation's strategic direction and resourcing as it juggles accounting and sustainability standard-setting simultaneously.
